title: Bridge Essay: In vitro neurons learn and exhibit sentience when embodied in a simulated game-world.
concept: Predictive processing — free energy principle, Bayesian brain, active inference, neural causality.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dependent Arising** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Predictive processing — free energy principle, Bayesian brain, active inference, neural causality.

**In vitro neurons learn and exhibit sentience when embodied in a simulated game-world.** - Neuron (2022). DOI: 10.1016/j.neuron.2022.09.001. PMID: 36228614. Integrating neurons into digital systems may enable performance infeasible with silicon alone. Here, we develop DishBrain, a system that harnesses the inherent adaptive computation of neurons in a structured environment. In vitro neural networks from human or rodent origins are integrated with in silico computing via a high-density multielectrode array. Through electrophysiological stimulation and recording, cultures are embedded in a simulated game-world, mimicking the arcade game "Pong." Apply
